signals.headline: NO NEW BREAKING DEVELOPMENTS ON US-IRAN CEASEFIRE IN APRIL 2026 | NO | LOW confidence**
signals.raw:
NO NEW BREAKING DEVELOPMENTS ON US-IRAN CEASEFIRE IN APRIL 2026 | NO | LOW confidence**

The US-Iran two-week ceasefire, announced by President Trump on April 7 and mediated by Pakistan, remains fragile but is not confirmed as newly resolved, extended, or collapsed as of April 15[1][2][3][4][5].

signals.headline: As of April 14, a US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz was imposed amid Iran's threats, ongoing Israeli strikes in
signals.raw:
As of April 14, a US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz was imposed amid Iran's threats, ongoing Israeli strikes in Lebanon, and Pakistan-proposed second-round talks before ceasefire expiry; no Iran attacks on US vessels reported, indicating partial hold[4][5][6].
